sentences.text: Smcsrind/Sscsrind is an ISA extension that extends the indirect CSR access mechanism originally defined as part of the https://github.com/riscv/riscv-aia[[.underline]Smaia/Ssaia extensions], in order to make it available for use by other extensions without creating an unnecessary dependence on Smaia/Ssaia.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: This extension confers two benefits:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: It provides a means to access an array of registers via CSRs without requiring allocation of large chunks of the limited CSR address space.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: It enables software to access each of an array of registers by index, without requiring a switch statement with a case for each register.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: The machine-level extension *Smcsrind* encompasses all added CSRs and all behavior modifications for a hart, over all privilege levels.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: For a supervisor-level environment, extension *Sscsrind* is essentially the same as Smcsrind except excluding the machine-level CSRs and behavior not directly visible to supervisor level.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: [width="100%",cols="15%,12%,12%,15%,46%",options="header",] | |*Number* |*Privilege* |*Width* |*Name* |*Description* |0x350 |MRW |XLEN |`miselect` |Machine indirect register select |0x351 |MRW |XLEN |`mireg` |Machine indirect register alias |0x352 |MRW |XLEN |`mireg2` |Machine indirect register alias 2 |0x353 |MRW |XLEN |`mireg3` |Machine indirect register alias 3 |0x355 |MRW |XLEN |`mireg4` |Machine indirect register alias 4 |0x356 |MRW |XLEN |`mireg5` |Machine indirect register alias 5 |0x357 |MRW |XLEN |`mireg6` |Machine indirect register alias 6 |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: The CSRs listed in the table above provide a window for accessing register state indirectly.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: The value of `miselect` determines which register is accessed upon read or write of each of the machine indirect alias CSRs (`mireg*`). `miselect` value ranges are allocated to dependent extensions, which specify the register state accessible via each `miregi` register, for each `miselect` value. `miselect` is a WARL register.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: The `miselect` register implements at least enough bits to support all implemented `miselect` values (corresponding to the implemented extensions that utilize `miselect`/`mireg*` to indirectly access register state).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: The `miselect` register may be read-only zero if there are no extensions implemented that utilize it.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: Values of `miselect` with the most-significant bit set (bit XLEN - 1 = 1) are designated only for custom use, presumably for accessing custom registers through the alias CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: Values of `miselect` with the most-significant bit clear are designated only for standard use and are reserved until allocated to a standard architecture extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: If XLEN is changed, the most-significant bit of `miselect` moves to the new position, retaining its value from before.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: The behavior upon accessing `mireg*` from M-mode, while `miselect` holds a value that is not implemented, is UNSPECIFIED.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: Attempts to access `mireg*` while `miselect` holds a number in an allocated and implemented range results in a specific behavior that, for each combination of `miselect` and `miregi`, is defined by the extension to which the `miselect` value is allocated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Machine-level CSRs

sentences.text: [width="100%",cols="15%,12%,12%,15%,46%",options="header",] | |*Number* |*Privilege* |*Width* |*Name* |*Description* |0x150 |SRW |XLEN |`siselect` |Supervisor indirect register select |0x151 |SRW |XLEN |`sireg` |Supervisor indirect register alias |0x152 |SRW |XLEN |`sireg2` |Supervisor indirect register alias 2 |0x153 |SRW |XLEN |`sireg3` |Supervisor indirect register alias 3 |0x155 |SRW |XLEN |`sireg4` |Supervisor indirect register alias 4 |0x156 |SRW |XLEN |`sireg5` |Supervisor indirect register alias 5 |0x157 |SRW |XLEN |`sireg6` |Supervisor indirect register alias 6 |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: The CSRs in the table above are required if S-mode is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: The `siselect` register will support the value range 0..0xFFF at a minimum. A future extension may define a value range outside of this minimum range.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Only if such an extension is implemented will `siselect` be required to support larger values.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Values of `siselect` with the most-significant bit set (bit XLEN - 1 = 1) are designated only for custom use, presumably for accessing custom registers through the alias CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Values of `siselect` with the most-significant bit clear are designated only for standard use and are reserved until allocated to a standard architecture extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: If XLEN is changed, the most-significant bit of `siselect` moves to the new position, retaining its value from before.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: The behavior upon accessing `sireg*` from M-mode or S-mode, while `siselect` holds a value that is not implemented at supervisor level, is UNSPECIFIED.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Otherwise, attempts to access `sireg*` from M-mode or S-mode while `siselect` holds a number in a standard-defined and implemented range result in specific behavior that, for each combination of `siselect` and `siregi`, is defined by the extension to which the `siselect` value is allocated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Note that the widths of `siselect` and `sireg*` are always the current XLEN rather than SXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Hence, for example, if MXLEN = 64 and SXLEN = 32, then these registers are 64 bits when the current privilege mode is M (running RV64 code) but 32 bits when the privilege mode is S (RV32 code).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: [width="100%",cols="15%,12%,12%,15%,46%",options="header",] | |*Number* |*Privilege* |*Width* |*Name* |*Description* |0x250 |HRW |XLEN |`vsiselect` |Virtual supervisor indirect register select
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: |0x251 |HRW |XLEN |`vsireg` |Virtual supervisor indirect register alias
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: |0x252 |HRW |XLEN |`vsireg2` |Virtual supervisor indirect register alias 2
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: |0x253 |HRW |XLEN |`vsireg3` |Virtual supervisor indirect register alias 3
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: |0x255 |HRW |XLEN |`vsireg4` |Virtual supervisor indirect register alias 4
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: |0x256 |HRW |XLEN |`vsireg5` |Virtual supervisor indirect register alias 5
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: |0x257 |HRW |XLEN |`vsireg6` |Virtual supervisor indirect register alias 6 |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: The CSRs in the table above are required if the hypervisor extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: These VS CSRs all match supervisor CSRs, and substitute for those supervisor CSRs when executing in a virtual machine (in VS-mode or VU-mode).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: The `vsiselect` register will support the value range 0..0xFFF at a minimum. A future extension may define a value range outside of this minimum range.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Only if such an extension is implemented will `vsiselect` be required to support larger values.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Values of `vsiselect` with the most-significant bit set (bit XLEN - 1 = 1) are designated only for custom use, presumably for accessing custom registers through the alias CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Values of `vsiselect` with the most-significant bit clear are designated only for standard use and are reserved until allocated to a standard architecture extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: If XLEN is changed, the most-significant bit of `vsiselect` moves to the new position, retaining its value from before.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: For alias CSRs `sireg*` and `vsireg*`, the hypervisor extension’s usual rules for when to raise a virtual-instruction exception (based on whether an instruction is HS-qualified) are not applicable.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: The rules given in this section for `sireg` and `vsireg` apply instead, unless overridden by the requirements specified in the section below, which take precedence over this section when extension Smstateen is also implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: A virtual-instruction exception is raised for attempts from VS-mode or VU-mode to directly access `vsiselect` or `vsireg*`, or attempts from VU-mode to access `siselect` or `sireg*`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: The behavior upon accessing `vsireg*` from M-mode or HS-mode, or accessing `sireg*` (really `vsireg*`) from VS-mode, while `vsiselect` holds a value that is not implemented at HS level, is UNSPECIFIED.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Otherwise, while `vsiselect` holds a number in a standard-defined and implemented range, attempts to access `vsireg*` from a sufficiently privileged mode, or to access `sireg*` (really `vsireg*`) from VS-mode, result in specific behavior that, for each combination of `vsiselect` and `vsiregi`, is defined by the extension to which the `vsiselect` value is allocated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Like `siselect` and `sireg*`, the widths of `vsiselect` and `vsireg*` are always the current XLEN rather than VSXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: Hence, for example, if HSXLEN = 64 and VSXLEN = 32, then these registers are 64 bits when accessed by a hypervisor in HS-mode (running RV64 code) but 32 bits for a guest OS in VS-mode (RV32 code).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Virtual Supervisor-level CSRs

sentences.text: If extension Smstateen is implemented together with Smcsrind, bit 60 of state-enable register `mstateen0` controls access to `siselect`, `sireg*`, `vsiselect`, and `vsireg*`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: When `mstateen0`[60]=0, an attempt to access one of these CSRs from a privilege mode less privileged than M-mode results in an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: As always, the state-enable CSRs do not affect the accessibility of any state when in M-mode, only in less privileged modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: For more explanation, see the documentation for extension Smstateen in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: Other extensions may specify that certain mstateen bits control access to registers accessed indirectly through `siselect` + `sireg*`, and/or `vsiselect` + `vsireg*`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: However, regardless of any other mstateen bits, if `mstateen0`[60] = 1, a virtual-instruction exception is raised as described in the previous section for all attempts from VS-mode or VU-mode to directly access `vsiselect` or `vsireg*`, and for all attempts from VU-mode to access `siselect` or `sireg*`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: If the hypervisor extension is implemented, the same bit is defined also in hypervisor CSR `hstateen0`, but controls access to only `siselect` and `sireg*` (really `vsiselect` and `vsireg*`), which is the state potentially accessible to a virtual machine executing in VS or VU-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: When `hstateen0`[60]=0 and `mstateen0`[60]=1, all attempts from VS or VU-mode to access `siselect` or `sireg*` raise a virtual-instruction exception, not an illegal-instruction exception, regardless of the value of `vsiselect` or any other mstateen bit.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: Extension Ssstateen is defined as the supervisor-level view of Smstateen.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs

sentences.text: Therefore, the combination of Sscsrind and Ssstateen incorporates the bit defined above for `hstateen0` but not that for `mstateen0`, since machine-level CSRs are not visible to supervisor level.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcsrind/Sscsrind" Indirect CSR Access, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Access control by the state-enable CSRs
